From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1979 studio album by Etta Jones

| If You Could See Me Now | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| [Studio album](/wiki/Album "Album") by [Etta Jones](/wiki/Etta_Jones "Etta Jones") | |
| Released | 1979 |
| Recorded | June 21, 1978 |
| Studio | [Van Gelder Studio](/wiki/Van_Gelder_Studio "Van Gelder Studio"), Englewood Cliffs, NJ |
| [Genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") | [Jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") |
| [Label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label") | [Muse](/wiki/Muse_Records "Muse Records") MR 5175 |
| [Producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer") | [Houston Person](/wiki/Houston_Person "Houston Person") |
| [Etta Jones](/wiki/Etta_Jones "Etta Jones") chronology | |
| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | *[My Mother's Eyes](/wiki/My_Mother%27s_Eyes_(Etta_Jones_album) "My Mother's Eyes (Etta Jones album)")* (1977) | ***If You Could See Me Now*** (1979) | *[Save Your Love for Me](/wiki/Save_Your_Love_for_Me "Save Your Love for Me")* (1981) | | |

***If You Could See Me Now*** is an album by vocalist [Etta Jones](/wiki/Etta_Jones "Etta Jones") that was recorded in 1978 and released on the [Muse](/wiki/Muse_Records "Muse Records") label.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=If_You_Could_See_Me_Now_(Etta_Jones_album)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Reception")]

*[DownBeat](/wiki/DownBeat "DownBeat")* assigned the album 5 stars.[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) Reviewer [Sam Freedman](/wiki/Sam_Freedman "Sam Freedman") wrote, "Jones doesn't flaunt her range, but devotes her energies to the power of decades-old lyrics. If a good singer tells a story. Jones *orates.* Except for an occasional slurred run of four or five words, she drenches each word with meaning . . . Throughout, the band is excellent. [Person](/wiki/Houston_Person "Houston Person") leaves his normal lusty grits-and-greens tenor for subtle, mid-register solos and answering twos and fours. [Phillips](/wiki/Sonny_Phillips "Sonny Phillips") is the smooth essence of what he calls “featherbedding.” The brief solos by drummer [Idris Muhammad](/wiki/Idris_Muhammad "Idris Muhammad") and vibist George Devens always matter. This may not be a ground-breaking album; Jones has been good lfor years. But *If You* *Could See Me Now* chronicles a woman at the height of her powers".[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)

Professional ratings

| Review scores | |
| --- | --- |
| Source | Rating |
| [AllMusic](/wiki/AllMusic "AllMusic") | StarStarStar[[4]](#cite_note-Allmusic-4) |
| *[DownBeat](/wiki/DownBeat "DownBeat")* | StarStarStarStarStar[[5]](#cite_note-5) |

## Track listing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=If_You_Could_See_Me_Now_(Etta_Jones_album)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Track listing")]

1. "[What a Little Moonlight Can Do](/wiki/What_a_Little_Moonlight_Can_Do "What a Little Moonlight Can Do")" ([Harry M. Woods](/wiki/Harry_M._Woods "Harry M. Woods")) – 3:59
2. "[Ghost of a Chance](/wiki/I_Don%27t_Stand_a_Ghost_of_a_Chance_with_You "I Don't Stand a Ghost of a Chance with You")" ([Victor Young](/wiki/Victor_Young "Victor Young"), [Ned Washington](/wiki/Ned_Washington "Ned Washington")) – 5:00
3. "I Saw Stars" ([Maurice Sigler](/wiki/Maurice_Sigler "Maurice Sigler"), [Al Goodhart](/wiki/Al_Goodhart "Al Goodhart"), [Al Hoffman](/wiki/Al_Hoffman "Al Hoffman")) – 4:24
4. "[If You Could See Me Now](/wiki/If_You_Could_See_Me_Now_(1946_song) "If You Could See Me Now (1946 song)")" ([Tadd Dameron](/wiki/Tadd_Dameron "Tadd Dameron")) – 6:35
5. "[I'm in the Mood for Love](/wiki/I%27m_in_the_Mood_for_Love "I'm in the Mood for Love")" ([Jimmy McHugh](/wiki/Jimmy_McHugh "Jimmy McHugh"), [Dorothy Fields](/wiki/Dorothy_Fields "Dorothy Fields")) – 5:43
6. "[It Could Happen to You](/wiki/It_Could_Happen_to_You_(song) "It Could Happen to You (song)")" ([Jimmy Van Heusen](/wiki/Jimmy_Van_Heusen "Jimmy Van Heusen"), [Johnny Burke](/wiki/Johnny_Burke_(lyricist) "Johnny Burke (lyricist)")) – 3:30
7. "[The Way We Were](/wiki/The_Way_We_Were_(song) "The Way We Were (song)")" ([Alan Bergman, Marilyn Bergman](/wiki/Alan_and_Marilyn_Bergman "Alan and Marilyn Bergman"), [Marvin Hamlisch](/wiki/Marvin_Hamlisch "Marvin Hamlisch")) – 4:17
8. "[Ain't Misbehavin'](/wiki/Ain%27t_Misbehavin%27_(song) "Ain't Misbehavin' (song)")" ([Andy Razaf](/wiki/Andy_Razaf "Andy Razaf"), [Fats Waller](/wiki/Fats_Waller "Fats Waller"), [Harry Brooks](/wiki/Harry_Brooks_(composer) "Harry Brooks (composer)")) – 4:09

## Personnel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=If_You_Could_See_Me_Now_(Etta_Jones_album)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personnel")]

* [Etta Jones](/wiki/Etta_Jones "Etta Jones") – vocals
* [Houston Person](/wiki/Houston_Person "Houston Person") – [tenor saxophone](/wiki/Tenor_saxophone "Tenor saxophone")
* [Sonny Phillips](/wiki/Sonny_Phillips "Sonny Phillips") – keyboards
* [Sam Jones](/wiki/Sam_Jones_(musician) "Sam Jones (musician)") – [bass](/wiki/Double_bass "Double bass")
* Larry Killian – percussion
* [Idris Muhammad](/wiki/Idris_Muhammad "Idris Muhammad") – drums
* George Devens – [vibraphone](/wiki/Vibraphone "Vibraphone")
* [Melvin Sparks](/wiki/Melvin_Sparks "Melvin Sparks") – guitar
